title: Color and Light
creator: James Gurney
description: An illustrated guide to light sources, color relationships, surfaces, atmosphere, visual perception, and practical observation. Although written for painters, it directly supports concept, environment, and lighting work.
useful: It connects color theory to observed physical situations, making it more practical than choosing palettes by mood alone.
audience: Concept artists, environment artists, lighting artists, and visually curious developers.
learning: Identify how light sources affect color
Use observation to make invented scenes more coherent

editorial.note: Included as adjacent craft knowledge for concept art, environment art, lighting, and coherent invented game spaces.
